read_plan.preview: [[Greyveil]] operates without formal municipal government, mayor, council, or administrative apparatus — yet 85,000 people receive water, dispose of waste, maintain roads, resolve disputes, coordinate markets, and sustai

Greyveil emerged from merchant settlement patterns rather than planned urban founding. No charter establishing governance, no formal incorporation as city, no royal decree creating administrative apparatus. Instead: merc

Auralis society leans toward a soft caste structure, where social standing is loosely tied to magical ability. This is not a rigid hierarchy of law — high-tier casters do not hold formal political office by virtue of the

Auralis embraces the full spectrum of [[Discipline|Disciplines]], including those that make other cultures uncomfortable. Experimentation with Mind and Soul disciplines is not openly banned and in certain scholarly or mi

The principal settlements of Auralis are [[Solhaven]] (the capital, built up coastal terraces on the western shore) and [[Emberfall]] (the interior research and agricultural center). Auralis has several additional port c
Solhaven approaches a million residents and is the most vertically stratified city in Solumora. Its terrace zones function as distinct social worlds stacked on each other.

**Independent scholars** operate throughout both kingdoms, conducting collaborative research that neither kingdom prioritizes internally. [[Kedn Rast]] ([[Control Tier|T3]]) exemplifies this institutional model, conducti

The numeric tier is the spell's **Flux handling requirement**. Each tier is roughly an order-of-magnitude jump in difficulty, capacity, stability, and precision. Every few tiers feels like a categorically different class

Greyveil itself is a frontier city in the practical sense: resourceful, self-reliant, and accustomed to being the last place that has something before the map runs out. It has a trade culture built on serving travelers g

Greyveil's practical leverage in northern logistics is the [[Greyveil-Wolfpoint Relay Track]]. Dispatch houses here handle packet intake, copy protocols, and relay scheduling for correspondence and compact high-value goo
Greyveil's current strain is increasingly visible in recurring service conflicts rather than headline crises.
The Board is funded by bulk-grain levies, storage licensing fees, and emergency reserve appropriations set by [[The Council of Auralis]].
It recruits warehouse planners, route schedulers, and documentation clerks from port and inland markets across [[Auralis]].

Large speculators pressure allocation policy during unstable seasons, and some expansion-focused operators resent reserve constraints that limit short-term export profit.

Rural Terravellian communities range from small family operations to coordinated settlements of several hundred people. The largest towns — trading posts at the intersection of river routes, established logging centers,

Hunting and trapping is not a marginal economic activity in rural [[Terravelle]]. It is primary. The forests are deep and rich, and they contain deer, elk, smaller game, fish in the rivers and lakes. A person with skill

Food preservation — [[Sigils]] carved into a space or into the structure of food itself — is not optional in rural [[Terravelle]]. The winter is long. A family must preserve enough meat, enough vegetables, enough grain t

A rural community in [[Terravelle]] maintains its own decisions about resource allocation, dispute resolution, and social structure. This does not mean lawlessness. It means that law is enacted locally and enforced throu

The courts struggle with [[Flux]]-enabled criminal activity because evidence standards designed for physical crimes do not adapt well to effects that can be concealed, manipulated, or destroyed through spellwork.

The **Greyveil Trade Quarter** is [[Greyveil]]'s commercial and market district, handling merchant operations, grain distribution, and trade coordination for northern[[Terravelle]]. With approximately 15,000 residents pl

The Quarter occupies the southeastern section of [[Greyveil]], positioned for optimal access to incoming wagon routes from the [[Northern Grain Corridor]] and outbound connections to southern [[Terravelle]] via the [[Mar
The Quarter's primary function: **converting distributed [[Northern Grain Corridor]] production into reliable urban food supply.**

Twelve established merchant houses maintain permanent Trade Quarter offices. These are **northern specialists** — operations focused on Greyveil-Wolfpoint-frontier trade rather than kingdom-wide commerce:

The **Northern Grain Corridor** is the agricultural supply network stretching from [[Terravelle]]'s northern farming belt to [[Greyveil]], providing the city's food base and creating the economic foundation for settlemen

The Corridor encompasses territory from fifty miles south of [[Greyveil]] extending west along the foothills and north into marginal farming zones at the edge of viable cultivation. Total agricultural population: approxi
Annual grain production: 4-5 million pounds (wheat, barley, rye in 60%/25%/15% distribution).
